heading: Enforcement, regulations, and scope
label: 1261 Enforcement, regulations, and scope
notes: 1948ActBased on sections 222, 223(b), 225 and 226 of title 27, U.S.C., 1940 ed., Intoxicating Liquors (, §§ 5, 10, , 1930).June 25, 1936, ch. 81549 Stat. 1929Changes were made in phraseology and arrangement.
1949ActThis section [section 31] corrects a typographical error in , U.S.C.section 1261 of title 18

Amendments2002—, which directed amendment of subsec. (a) generally, was executed by amending text of section generally to reflect the probable intent of Congress and the amendment by , see below. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “The Secretary of the Treasury shall enforce the provisions of this chapter. Regulations to carry out its provisions shall be prescribed by the Commissioner of Internal Revenue with the approval of the Secretary of the Treasury.”Pub. L. 107–296Pub. L. 107–273struck out subsec. (a) designation and subsec. (b) which read as follows: “This chapter shall not apply to the Canal Zone.”Pub. L. 107–2731949—Subsec. (b). Act , substituted subsection designation “(b)” for “(d)”.May 24, 1949

Effective Date of 2002 AmendmentAmendment by effective 60 days after , see , set out as an Effective Date note under , Domestic Security.Pub. L. 107–296section 4 of Pub. L. 107–296section 101 of Title 6Nov. 25, 2002
